text: Yes - Stage B manufactures failure dynamics cheaply by perturbing correct proofs (decoy-tactic replacement, deletion, no-op insertion, neighbor-replace) and recording the faithful Lean outcome and error_class. At fixture scale this yields a realistic mix (solved/error/still_valid across error classes). Recording failure transitions is the dataset's headline novelty - no existing Lean corpus models dynamics including failure.

comments._self: CAVEAT (audit): scope. The 'realistic mix' is demonstrated only at TOY/FIXTURE scale (e-0001: ~66 records; Mathlib scale-up explicitly blocked on disk, model/baseline/eval not built), so 'manufactures failure dynamics cheaply' is established as a pipeline-feasibility claim, not a scale claim. Separately, the headline-novelty assertion 'no existing Lean corpus models dynamics including failure' is UNCITED -- treat as an unverified literature claim pending a survey of existing Lean/ITP corpora.
